CloudVault Systems

Enterprise-grade cloud infrastructure for modern enterprises

LEAD CLOUD ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-first, some travel for client engagement) · Full-time · $190,000 - $240,000 USD + equity

We are seeking an experienced Lead Cloud Engineer to lead our AWS Infrastructure team and mentor

a group of 5-7 cloud engineers. In this role, you will drive architectural decisions, oversee cloud

migration projects for enterprise clients, manage infrastructure-as-code practices, and establish

technical standards across the organization. You will report to the VP of Engineering and be responsible

for team development, project delivery, and technical excellence. This is an ideal opportunity for a

senior engineer ready to transition into technical leadership.

ABOUT US

CloudVault Systems is a mid-stage cloud infrastructure and DevOps consulting firm specializing in

designing, deploying, and optimizing multi-cloud environments for Fortune 500 companies and high-
growth startups. Founded in 2016, we help organizations migrate to AWS, optimize their cloud spend,

and build resilient, scalable infrastructure. Our team of 150+ engineers across 4 offices serves over 200
enterprise clients globally.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 cloud engineers, providing technical guidance, code reviews, and

career development plans

Design and implement cloud infrastructure solutions on AWS using Terraform and

CloudFormation, ensuring scalability and cost-efficiency

Oversee multi-million dollar cloud migration projects for enterprise clients, including architecture

design and implementation oversight

Establish and enforce infrastructure-as-code standards, CI/CD best practices, and security
policies across the engineering organization

Conduct cost optimization audits for client environments (EC2 rightsizing, Reserved Instance
planning, Lambda optimization) and lead implementation of savings strategies

Design and manage VPC architectures, networking security groups, IAM policies, and cross-
account access patterns for enterprise deployments

Collaborate with sales and customer success teams to estimate cloud infrastructure budgets and
technical feasibility for new opportunities

Drive improvements to team processes, tooling, and deployment pipelines; identify and resolve

technical bottlenecks

 Stay current with AWS service evolution and emerging cloud technologies; evaluate adoption of

new services like ECS, EKS, and serverless patterns

REQUIRED

6+ years of hands-on cloud engineering experience, with 3+ years on AWS at scale

Demonstrated leadership experience managing or mentoring engineering teams of 3+ people

Expert-level proficiency with AWS core services: EC2, VPC, IAM, Lambda, RDS, S3, and

CloudWatch

Advanced experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform and/or CloudFormation); ability

to design reusable modules and best practices

Strong networking fundamentals (VPCs, subnetting, security groups, routing, NAT gateways,

VPN)

Proven track record optimizing cloud costs; experience with AWS Cost Explorer, Trusted Advisor,

and capacity planning

Excellent communication skills with ability to explain complex technical concepts to both engineers
and non-technical stakeholders

NICE TO HAVE

AWS certifications (Solutions Architect Professional or DevOps Engineer Professional)

Experience with Kubernetes, Docker, and container orchestration in production environments

Familiarity with CI/CD tools (GitLab, GitHub Actions, Jenkins) and deployment automation

Experience with monitoring and observability tools (DataDog, New Relic, Prometheus)

Background in security-focused cloud architecture and compliance (SOC2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS)

Open-source contributions or published technical writing on cloud infrastructure topics

TECH STACK

AWS (EC2, VPC, IAM, Lambda, RDS, S3, CloudWatch, ECS, EKS)

  Terraform   CloudFormation   Docker & Kubernetes

Python/Bash scripting   GitLab CI/CD   AWS Cost Explorer & Trusted Advisor

  DataDog or New Relic   Jenkins   Ansible

BENEFITS

Competitive salary with annual performance bonuses and equity grants

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% employer coverage

401(k) matching up to 6% with immediate vesting

Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 20 days recommended, plus 10 company holidays

Professional development budget of $3,000/year for courses, certifications, and conferences

Flexible work arrangement: remote-first with optional office access in San Francisco

Parental leave (12 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 4 weeks for secondary)

 Mental health support via EAP and subsidized gym memberships

HOW TO APPLY

Please submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your leadership experience and favorite cloud

project, and links to any public GitHub repositories or technical writing to

careers@cloudvaultsystems.com. Please use subject line 'Lead Cloud Engineer - [Your Name]'. We

review applications on a rolling basis and aim to respond within 5 business days. Initial screening calls

typically take 30 minutes, followed by a technical interview with team members and a final conversation

with the VP of Engineering.

Background in security-focused cloud architecture and compliance (SOC2, HIPAA, PCI-DSS)
